arguments.task: Research Aircall using official first-party pages only where possible. Find and open Aircall developer/API documentation, authentication docs, pricing or plans pages, any rate limit docs, and any official mention of MCP or an official GitHub/MCP registry page. Extract exact URLs and the facts needed to determine: one-line product description, auth methods for API, whether access is self-serve or gated/paid, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, and any notable signals about public API availability. Return concise findings with source URLs.

input.thought: Start an official-source browser research session for Aircall.

arguments.task: Search GitHub for Aircall MCP server or Model Context Protocol repository. Prefer official Aircall org results, then notable third-party MCP repositories if clearly about Aircall. Also check MCP registries or official docs if surfaced. Return whether an MCP appears to exist and the exact URLs found.

input.thought: Run a parallel browser search focused on MCP existence for Aircall.

raw_result.description: Cloud-based business phone and call center platform with APIs for calling, users, teams, contacts, and webhooks.
raw_result.auth_notes: Aircall developer docs state customers authenticate API requests with Basic Auth, while partners can use OAuth 2.0.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public API docs are available, but pricing evidence indicates API access is tied to paid Aircall plans (Essentials, Professional, Custom), not a free self-serve developer tier.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documentation includes resources such as calls, users, teams, numbers, contacts, tags, comments, messages/inbox-related resources, integrations, and webhooks; breadth appears sufficient for a first agent toolkit.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Aircall MCP server was verified from the gathered evidence; browser findings indicate no MCP repository in the official Aircall GitHub org and no listing found in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repo. Third-party MCP repos may exist, but that remains unverified here.

raw_result.blocker: Paid Aircall plan required for practical API use; MCP availability is unverified and some access details may depend on customer vs partner status.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Aircall API docs indicate rate limiting of 120 requests per minute per company.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exists with API reference and auth/rate-limit sections. Pricing page indicates plans start around $30/license/month billed annually with a 3-license minimum, and Custom mentions developer support. Browser-gathered evidence suggests official GitHub presence but no verified official MCP server.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The exact wording and scope of plan-based API access and MCP non-existence were inferred from browser-extracted findings rather than directly quoting all relevant page text. Official absence of MCP cannot be conclusively proven from available evidence alone.

result.description: Cloud-based business phone and call center platform with APIs for calling, users, teams, contacts, and webhooks.
result.auth_notes: Aircall developer docs state customers authenticate API requests with Basic Auth, while partners can use OAuth 2.0.
result.gating_reason: Public API docs are available, but pricing evidence indicates API access is tied to paid Aircall plans (Essentials, Professional, Custom), not a free self-serve developer tier.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documentation includes resources such as calls, users, teams, numbers, contacts, tags, comments, messages/inbox-related resources, integrations, and webhooks; breadth appears sufficient for a first agent toolkit.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Aircall MCP server was verified from the gathered evidence; browser findings indicate no MCP repository in the official Aircall GitHub org and no listing found in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repo. Third-party MCP repos may exist, but that remains unverified here.

result.rate_limit_notes: Aircall API docs indicate rate limiting of 120 requests per minute per company.
result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exists with API reference and auth/rate-limit sections. Pricing page indicates plans start around $30/license/month billed annually with a 3-license minimum, and Custom mentions developer support. Browser-gathered evidence suggests official GitHub presence but no verified official MCP server.
